title: pisophaca famelica
intro: Pisophaca famelica, also known as the Utah pea or Utah birdspur, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The herbal preparations of Pisophaca famelica typically involve drying and infusing the plant's leaves and stems in hot water or oil to create a tea or tincture for medicinal purposes.
